text.title: Python Async Patterns — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-async is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.description: time.sleep / requests.get / sync DB inside async def.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Loop stalls; every concurrent coroutine freezes until the call returns. Fix: Replace with asyncio.sleep, httpx.AsyncClient, asyncpg/aiosqlite.

antipattern.description: task = asyncio.create_task(coro()) result thrown away or task = ... discarded.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: GC may collect the task before completion → RuntimeError 'Task was destroyed but it is pending'. Fix: Store the task in a list/set or use TaskGroup.

antipattern.description: asyncio.gather(*tasks) without return_exceptions on a critical fan-out.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: First failure leaves siblings running as orphans, leaking connections. Fix: Use TaskGroup; or gather with return_exceptions=True and explicit per-result handling.

antipattern.description: Django ORM call inside async def without sync_to_async wrapper.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Blocks the loop indefinitely; tests look like hangs. Fix: Wrap with asgiref.sync.sync_to_async or use Django's async ORM API.
